response.justification: Strong technical leader with proven mentorship and large-scale systems experience, but fails to meet a critical minimum qualification: the role requires 8 years of C++ programming, while the resume documents only ~1 year of C++ work (National Instruments, 2014-2015). Additionally, the candidate's career is primarily web/backend engineering (Node.js, TypeScript, React, AWS CDK) rather than cloud compute platform architecture, which is central to this mid-senior leadership role. While the candidate demonstrates excellent software craftsmanship and progression to staff-level positions, the technical foundation gaps are too significant for a platform engineering leadership role.
strengths: Demonstrated technical leadership progression: Staff Engineer at Google, Meta, and Altom with proven mentoring through workshops, code katas, and team guidance
Experience with large-scale distributed systems: handled millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby; automated platform processing millions of transactions at Meta
Full-stack technical capability and architecture understanding: cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes), microservices design, and ability to own complex technical projects end-to-end
concerns: Critical C++ shortfall: requires 8 years but resume shows only ~1 year documented (National Instruments 2014-2015); primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, D, not C++

Cloud infrastructure platform depth unclear: career focuses on web/backend and DevOps tooling (CI/CD, Docker) rather than cloud compute platform architecture, storage systems, or network infrastructure design

Recent role misalignment: current Google position (since April 2025) emphasizes full-stack and AI-agent tooling, not cloud compute platform leadership; Meta role was pricing optimization, not platform engineering
